metadata.tagline: Bolster your mind's concentration and attention
metadata.purpose: Detailed visual dashboard with FULL scripts and guidance

approaches.script: (Smile and gesture toward their work) "Hey, sorry to bother you! I can see you're grinding away over there — I totally get it. I'm [Your Name]. I actually work with a product called Focus that helps people stay locked in and clear-headed without caffeine or stimulants. Do you ever struggle with staying dialed in when you really need to concentrate?"

"Hey [Friend's Name]! Remember how you were telling me about all the stuff you're juggling right now — the projects, the deadlines, all of it? I started using this thing called Focus, and honestly my concentration has been on another level. It's completely drug-free, just uses vibrotactile technology on a patch. Have you ever tried anything like that for mental clarity?"

"Hey [Name]! I saw your post about brain fog and trying to power through long study sessions — I've so been there. I've been using a product called Focus that supports concentration and mental clarity without any pills or caffeine. It's been a game-changer for me. Would you be open to hearing how it works?"

"Hi [Prospect Name], I'm [Your Name]. [Referrer's Name] suggested I reach out — they mentioned you've been looking for ways to sharpen your focus and stay productive without relying on energy drinks or supplements. I work with a product called Focus that does exactly that using drug-free vibrotactile technology. Would you be open to a quick chat about how it could help you?"

"Great event, right? So what do you do? (Listen and respond genuinely.) That sounds like it takes serious concentration! I actually help people level up their mental clarity and focus with a product called Focus — it's a drug-free patch that uses vibrotactile technology. Have you ever noticed your focus dropping off when you need it most?"

questions.question: What does a typical productive day look like for you, and what helps you get into that 'flow state'?
What are some of the biggest challenges you face when it comes to staying focused, maintaining concentration, or achieving mental clarity, especially when you need it most?
When you're not able to focus as well as you'd like, how does that impact your work, studies, or even your personal life?
What have you tried in the past to improve your concentration or mental performance, and what were your experiences with those solutions?
If you could snap your fingers and have perfect, sustained focus, what would that enable you to achieve or change in your daily life?
When it comes to your cognitive performance and mental well-being, what's most important for you to improve right now?
What's your current routine or 'go-to' when you really need to buckle down and concentrate on a task?
How do your energy levels typically affect your ability to focus throughout the day, and do you experience any mid-day slumps?
If you were to imagine an ideal solution for consistent focus and mental clarity, what would it look like or feel like for you?
As you consider solutions for improving your focus, what criteria are most important to you in making that decision?

content.problem: You sit down to work and within minutes your mind is somewhere else — checking your phone, jumping between tabs, struggling to hold a single thought. Brain fog, constant distractions, and mental fatigue have become your daily reality. It feels like no matter how hard you try, you just can't lock in when it matters most.
content.agitate: When you can't focus, it doesn't just slow you down — it chips away at your confidence, your deadlines, and your peace of mind. You end up frustrated, relying on caffeine or sheer willpower, and the cycle just repeats itself day after day. Meanwhile, the mental fatigue piles up and starts bleeding into your relationships and your sense of self-worth. {{discovery_callback}}
content.solve: That's where Focus comes in. It uses Vibrotactile Technology — VTT — to support the neural pathways your brain relies on for concentration and mental clarity, completely drug-free. No pills, no stimulants, nothing entering your bloodstream — just a gentle signal that helps your mind lock in and stay sharp when you need it most.

objections.response: I get it — let me ask you this. How much are you currently spending on nootropics, focus supplements, or even extra coffee to power through brain fog? Most people are spending $60-$150 a month on pills and drinks that still leave them with jitters or crashes. Focus gives you sustained mental clarity for less than that, with zero side effects. What's the cost of another month of lost productivity and scattered thinking?
objections.psychology: Reframe cost by comparing to existing nootropic/supplement spend and the hidden cost of poor focus

objections.response: Of course — and honestly, the fact that you're thinking carefully about it tells me you value making smart decisions. What's the main thing on your mind? Is it whether Focus will actually help you concentrate better than what you're doing now, or is it more about understanding how VTT technology works? I want to make sure you feel confident.
objections.psychology: Compliment their thoughtfulness while isolating whether the hesitation is about efficacy or mechanism

objections.response: That's smart — decisions like this are easier together. What do you think their main question would be? A lot of families find that both partners benefit from Focus — one for work deadlines, the other for managing the household. It's not just one person's productivity that improves. Would it be helpful if I put together a quick overview they can look at?
objections.psychology: Position Focus as a shared household benefit that improves productivity for the whole family

objections.response: Totally fair question. Focus uses Vibrotactile Technology — it doesn't put any chemicals in your body. Instead, it works through your nervous system to support your brain's natural ability to concentrate. No pills, no caffeine, nothing entering your bloodstream. What would make you feel confident — would you want to wear it during a work session and see if you notice a difference in how locked-in you feel?
objections.psychology: Let them define proof by proposing a real-world concentration test tied to their daily work

objections.response: I hear that a lot — most people have tried nootropics, Adderall alternatives, caffeine stacking, all sorts of focus hacks. What happened? Usually it's the jitters, the crash, or it just stopped working after a few weeks. Focus is completely different because VTT doesn't introduce any substance into your body — it works with your nervous system directly. What was the biggest letdown with what you tried before?
objections.psychology: Differentiate VTT from every supplement and stimulant-based focus product they've already tried and abandoned

objections.response: No worries at all. Just curious — are you genuinely satisfied with your concentration and mental clarity right now, or is it more that you've tried so many things that didn't work that you've stopped looking? If it's the second one, I totally understand — but Focus works through a completely different mechanism than anything else out there. What would it mean for you to have reliable, on-demand mental clarity?
objections.psychology: Distinguish between true satisfaction and learned helplessness from past focus product failures

objections.response: That actually makes this more relevant, not less. Focus is a peel-and-stick patch — takes 5 seconds, no pills to remember, no drinks to mix. And here's the thing: if you could get even one extra hour of real, deep concentration per day, what would that be worth? People who are busiest are usually the ones who benefit from Focus the most.
objections.psychology: Reframe busyness as the exact reason Focus is valuable — more focused hours means more done in less time

objections.response: Great question — what are you comparing it to? Most focus products — whether it's nootropic stacks, caffeine pills, or prescription options — put a substance into your body that can cause jitters, dependency, or a crash. Focus uses VTT to work with your nervous system and support your brain's natural concentration pathways. Nothing enters your bloodstream, no tolerance buildup. Are you looking for a quick spike in alertness, or sustained mental clarity you can count on every day?
objections.psychology: Highlight that VTT is a fundamentally different mechanism vs. all chemical-based focus competitors with no tolerance buildup

6_closing.title: 🏆 5 CLOSING TECHNIQUES
6_closing.pre_close: Always ask: 'Do you have any questions?' + 'Does this make sense?'

techniques.name: ASSUMPTIVE
techniques.script: Awesome — so you're ready to have laser-sharp concentration and power through your work without the brain fog. Let's get your Focus order on the way — what's the best shipping address?
techniques.when: They've described struggling with concentration, brain fog, or scattered thinking and are clearly bought in on the solution

techniques.name: ALTERNATIVE
techniques.script: Would you like to start with a 30-day supply to see how it transforms your work sessions, or grab the 90-day pack so you're locked in through the whole semester or project cycle?
techniques.when: They're convinced Focus is the answer but deliberating on quantity — tie the options to their work or study timeline

techniques.name: URGENCY
techniques.script: We have a promotion running right now that's ending soon — and think about it, every day you're fighting brain fog is another day of lost productivity you can't get back. Want to lock this in so you can start powering through your work with real clarity this week?
techniques.when: They agree Focus sounds perfect but are dragging their feet — remind them that brain fog is costing them output every single day

techniques.name: SUMMARY
techniques.script: So here's where we landed — you want laser-sharp concentration, no more brain fog, the ability to power through deep work or study sessions, and you want it drug-free with no jitters. That's Focus in a nutshell. Ready to make it happen?
techniques.when: Complex conversation covering multiple focus and productivity pain points — summarize everything before closing

techniques.name: REFERRAL
techniques.script: Now that you're about to experience what real mental clarity feels like, who else comes to mind — a classmate, a colleague, maybe someone on your team who's always battling brain fog — who'd benefit from this?
techniques.when: After they've purchased and are excited — tap into their network of students, professionals, or coworkers who struggle with focus

7_followup.title: 📅 FOLLOW-UP SEQUENCE
7_followup.goal: Build relationship → Get testimonials → Generate referrals

sequence.template: Great connecting! Here's a quick guide to get the most from your patch. Let me know if questions pop up!
How are you feeling with your patches? Noticed any difference yet? I'd love to hear!
One week in! Are you feeling the benefits? Would you be open to sharing your experience?
Hope you're loving the results! Time to reorder soon. Who else might benefit from this?
